annotation.text: The COBRA Register is an official publication of the City of Baltimore published monthly by the Department of Legislative Reference. Each edition of the Register contains proposed regulations that are up for public comment during the month of publication and serves as the central document for members of the public to review and provide feedback on those regulations. While bills heard on the Council floor are debated by Councilmembers, regulations allow Baltimore residents to directly participate in policymaking through the public comment period. When a proposed City regulation is published in the COBRA Register, members of the public have 30 calendar days to offer comments and feedback to authoring Agencies on the content of the regulation.
The public comment period is a critical part of the creation and enactment of regulations, a process called "promulgation." Make your voice heard by reviewing this month's COBRA Register below!
